title: Min/Max properties of Set types should be preferred instead of the Enumerable extension methods
description: Both the Enumerable.Max extension method and the SortedSet<T>.Max property can be used to find the maximum value in a SortedSet<T>. However, SortedSet<T>.Max is much faster than Enumerable.Max. For small collections, the performance difference may be minor, but for large collections, it can be noticeable.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

never_trigger_on: LINQ chains (Where, Select, OrderBy followed by Min/Max)
logic.reasoning: Detects calls to Min() and Max() extension methods on SortedSet<T> and SortedDictionary<T,U>.Keys where the corresponding properties should be used for O(1) performance instead of O(n). Targets both CallExpression and Assignment nodes to catch direct calls and variable assignments. Excludes LINQ operations, logging, and regular collections where the methods are appropriate.
